./website/layouts/index.html:85:            <p>Agents run in dedicated git worktrees — no shared state, no context bleed. Results merge cleanly or are preserved for review.</p>

./website/content/why/_index.md:61:An agent is treated as an untrusted process that happens to be useful, not as a trusted teammate. Each run executes in an isolated worktree with a narrow contract; access to credentials, networks, and shared state is scoped to what the bead actually needs. The blast radius of a bad run stays bounded.
./website/content/features/_index.md:43:`ddx work` drains the bead queue. It picks the next ready bead, runs the configured harness inside an isolated git worktree, captures evidence, and merges the result back when acceptance criteria pass. Failing or timed-out runs are preserved as refs for inspection rather than discarded.
./website/content/features/_index.md:50:Because each iteration runs in its own worktree, parallel runs are safe and a failed run never poisons the main branch.
./website/content/features/_index.md:66:Every execution writes a bundle under `.ddx/executions/<timestamp>-<hash>/` with the prompt, the agent transcript, token and cost telemetry, the diff produced, and the merge outcome. Evidence stays in the repository, attached to the commit, so future reviewers (human or model) can reconstruct what happened without rerunning the agent.

./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:52:{"id":"corpus-368f5152b39b","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"layer-2 design: should execute-bead workers create beads in-band?","description":"During investigation of ddx-7eab13a6 (bead-id resolver worktree bug), a design question surfaced: is it correct for execute-bead workers to call `ddx bead create` directly in-band?\n\nThree alternatives:\n\n(a) Append to parent bead. Worker appends discovered sub-tasks as structured items in the parent's notes or a new discovered-subtask event. The parent becomes the durable record; future runs or the operator can decompose.\n\n(b) Surface via result. Agent emits a structured discovered_subtasks: [...] field in result.json; the work / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.\n\n(c) Create beads in-band (current behavior). Worker calls ddx bead create directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue on over-decomposition.\n\nToday's behavior is (c) but undocumented. One worker spawned 11 read-coverage children in a single run (ddx-44236615), all P0, without operator review. The work may be valid but the pattern needs a documented position.\n\nAdditionally, 22 existing beads have malformed IDs of the form .execute-bead-wt-\u003cparent\u003e-\u003ctimestamp\u003e-\u003crandom\u003e-\u003chex\u003e (caused by the layer-1 bug fixed in ddx-7eab13a6). A mass-repair or close+refile decision should be made once the design position is set.\n\nScope: pick (a), (b), or (c) and document in a spec amendment (FEAT-006 or FEAT-010 if applicable); record the rationale. Also decide whether to repair the 22 existing malformed-ID beads.","labels":["area:bead","area:agent","kind:design"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-d3a4f89c","title":"layer-2 design: should execute-bead workers create beads in-band?","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"During investigation of ddx-7eab13a6 (bead-id resolver worktree bug), a design question surfaced: is it correct for execute-bead workers to call `ddx bead create` directly in-band?\n\nThree alternatives:\n\n(a) Append to parent bead. The parent becomes the durable record; future runs or the operator can decompose.\n\n(b) Surface via result. Agent emits a structured discovered_subtasks: [...] field in result.json; the work / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.\n\n(c) Create beads in-band (current behavior). Worker calls ddx bead create directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue on over-decomposition.\n\nToday's behavior is (c) but undocumented. One worker spawned 11 read-coverage children in a single run (ddx-44236615), all P0, without operator review. The work may be valid but the pattern needs a documented position.\n\nAdditionally, 22 existing beads have malformed IDs of the form .execute-bead-wt-\u003cparent\u003e-\u003ctimestamp\u003e-\u003crandom\u003e-\u003chex\u003e (caused by the layer-1 bug fixed in ddx-7eab13a6). A mass-repair or close+refile decision should be made once the design position is set.\n\nScope: pick (a), (b), or (c) and document in a spec amendment (FEAT-006 or FEAT-010 if applicable); record the rationale. Also decide whether to repair the 22 existing malformed-ID beads.","labels":["area:bead","area:agent","kind:design"]}}
./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:56:{"id":"corpus-399a45730bb9","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"BUG: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI upstream-service error aborts agent without classification","description":"## Symptom\n\nSessions in `Projects/ddx/.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` aborted mid-stream with:\n\n```\nagent: upstream-service error: received error while streaming: {\"message\":\"Error in iterating prediction stream: NotImplementedError: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI\"}\n```\n\nObserved against `surface=embedded-openai`. The agent surfaces this as a generic \"upstream-service error\" and aborts the session.\n\n## Why it matters\n\nThis is a **server-capability** error — the local OpenAI-compat server (probably mlx_lm / lmstudio variant) cannot handle a quantized rotating KV cache for the route-target+config combination. The user can fix it (turn off KV quant; use a different cache type; pick a different route-target). Today the error is opaque, so the user just sees a stream abort and may retry endlessly.\n\n## Proposed fix\n\nIn `upstream-service/openai` (or wherever the streaming SSE error is decoded):\n\n1. Detect the `NotImplementedError: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI` substring (or any `NotImplementedError` from the upstream-service) and classify it as a structured error, not a transport error.\n2. Emit a clear human message: \"Local server does not support quantized rotating KV cache for this route-target. Disable KV quantization on the server, or use a route-target/config it supports.\"\n3. Mark the error non-retryable in the routing layer — endless retries waste compute and the failure is deterministic.\n4. Optional: add a routing hint so this upstream-service+route-target combo is skipped on subsequent dispatches in the same session.\n\n## Related\n\nCounterpart bead being filed in lucebox-hub/dflash to add this as a known failure mode the bench should be able to provoke deliberately (it currently passes `enable_thinking: false` to avoid the related \"reasoning stall\" path, but doesn't surface KV-cache-incompatibility paths).","acceptance":"1. When a streaming response contains NotImplementedError: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI (or any NotImplementedError from the upstream-service), the agent emits a structured error with a stable code (e.g. UPSTREAM-SERVICE_CAPABILITY_MISSING) and a human-readable message naming the missing capability.\n2. The error is non-retryable in the routing layer — agent does not silently retry on the same upstream-service+route-target.\n3. A unit test fixture replays a recorded SSE stream containing this error and asserts the structured classification.\n4. Behavior verified against ddx replay of the failing session in sessions.jsonl.","labels":["kind:bug","area:upstream-service"],"bead":{"id":"agent-a8915e01","title":"BUG: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI upstream-service error aborts agent without classification","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"## Symptom\n\nSessions in `Projects/ddx/.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` aborted mid-stream with:\n\n```\nagent: upstream-service error: received error while streaming: {\"message\":\"Error in iterating prediction stream: NotImplementedError: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI\"}\n```\n\nObserved against `surface=embedded-openai`. The user can fix it (turn off KV quant; use a different cache type; pick a different route-target). Today the error is opaque, so the user just sees a stream abort and may retry endlessly.\n\n## Proposed fix\n\nIn `upstream-service/openai` (or wherever the streaming SSE error is decoded):\n\n1. Detect the `NotImplementedError: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI` substring (or any `NotImplementedError` from the upstream-service) and classify it as a structured error, not a transport error.\n2. Emit a clear human message: \"Local server does not support quantized rotating KV cache for this route-target. Disable KV quantization on the server, or use a route-target/config it supports.\"\n3. Mark the error non-retryable in the routing layer — endless retries waste compute and the failure is deterministic.\n4. Optional: add a routing hint so this upstream-service+route-target combo is skipped on subsequent dispatches in the same session.\n\n## Related\n\nCounterpart bead being filed in lucebox-hub/dflash to add this as a known failure mode the bench should be able to provoke deliberately (it currently passes `enable_thinking: false` to avoid the related \"reasoning stall\" path, but doesn't surface KV-cache-incompatibility paths).","acceptance":"1. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:59:{"id":"corpus-3ca7318389e5","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Switch the default Playwright e2e runner to a Go-server fixture","description":"## Context\n\nThe frontend Playwright suite under `cli/internal/server/frontend` currently starts `bun run preview` on port 4173, which serves static SvelteKit output without the Go DDx backend. The parent e2e suite now includes direct API tests and route/page tests that require real DDx server data. TC-008 in `e2e/app.spec.ts` calls `/api/health`, `/api/documents`, `/api/beads`, `/api/beads/status`, `/api/personas`, and `/api/docs/graph`; those endpoints return 404 under static preview but return 200 from the Go server.\n\nBuild a fixture-backed default Playwright runner that boots the Go DDx server on a deterministic local port and points Playwright at it. The fixture must be self-contained so e2e runs do not read the developer's home config or the repository's live `.ddx/` state.\n\n## In-scope files\n\n- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.config.ts` — change the default `webServer` to run the Go DDx server (`go run ./cli` from the repo root or a built `ddx` binary) on a deterministic port, set `use.baseURL` to that server, and keep any static-only preview coverage separate from the default backend-backed project.\n- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/` — add a fixture project directory containing minimal `.ddx/beads.jsonl` data with open, closed, ready, and blocked beads; a tiny `docs/` library; and any config needed by the server.\n- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/app.spec.ts` — only TC-008 API-test alignment if the fixture data requires narrow expectation updates.\n\n## Out-of-scope\n\n- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.demo.config.ts`.\n- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts`.\n- Go server source under `cli/internal/server/`; the API is assumed correct.\n- Frontend components or routes.\n- Migrating TC-001 through TC-007 page tests; those are separate child beads.\n\n## Rollback / cleanup\n\nDo not leave a half-populated fixture directory. Do not make tests pass by skipping or deleting them. The default test runner must use a temp copy or otherwise isolated fixture workspace, not the developer's actual repository checkout or `$HOME`.","acceptance":"cd cli/internal/server/frontend \u0026\u0026 bun run test:e2e --grep TC-008\ncd cli/internal/server/frontend \u0026\u0026 ! grep -rE '\\$HOME|~/' e2e/fixtures/\ncd cli/internal/server/frontend \u0026\u0026 grep -E 'webServer|baseURL|ddx|go run' playwright.config.ts\ncd cli/internal/server/frontend \u0026\u0026 git diff --exit-code -- playwright.demo.config.ts e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts","labels":["area:test","area:frontend","kind:test-infra","phase:test"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-fc6e5b14","title":"Switch the default Playwright e2e runner to a Go-server fixture","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"## Context\n\nThe frontend Playwright suite under `cli/internal/server/frontend` currently starts `bun run preview` on port 4173, which serves static SvelteKit output without the Go DDx backend. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:63:{"id":"corpus-42801b4c2e9b","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"agent: hardcoded 15-minute DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout kills thinking-route-target requests; not configurable","description":"Source: cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:30\n  const DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout = 15 * time.Minute\n\nThis is hardcoded, not surfaced through .ddx/config.yaml or any flag, and is applied uniformly to every upstream-service regardless of route-target class. It is set on every service path:\n  - cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:116  Upstream-serviceTimeout: DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout\n  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:153           Upstream-serviceTimeout: DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout\n\nThe comment justifies it as 'Defeats RC4 of ddx-0a651925: a stalled TCP socket that has delivered headers but stopped emitting body bytes would otherwise pin a goroutine until the outer wall-clock (3h) frees it.'\n\nBut that scenario is already handled by a separate idle-read timeout immediately below, on line 34:\n  const DefaultUpstream-serviceIdleReadTimeout = 5 * time.Minute\n\nThe 5-minute idle-read timeout is exactly the right tool for the stalled-TCP-socket case (no body bytes for 5 min → fail). The 15-minute wall-clock cap on a single request is the WRONG tool — it kills any legitimately long single Chat call regardless of streaming health.\n\nConcrete impact observed today (2026-04-29 axon repo):\n\n1. axon-db7a6d0a worker (qwen3.6-35b-a3b on bragi via lmstudio)\n   - status: no_changes\n   - duration_ms: 1,762,081 (29 min total — one timeout + retry)\n   - log: 'WARN upstream-service error, retrying attempt=1 err=\"upstream-service request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s\" delay=1s'\n   - Retry consumed another full 15 min, also timed out, no commits produced.\n\n2. axon-80979cb8 + axon-db7a6d0a + axon-ab2e52e0 (3-worker cascade re-launch)\n   - All 3 hit 'upstream-service request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s' on the first attempt\n   - User killed the run before retries could waste another 15 min × 3 each\n\nWhy thinking route-targets hit this:\nqwen3.6-35b-a3b and similar 'thinking' / chain-of-thought reasoning route-targets can spend 5–10+ minutes generating reasoning tokens before producing the first body delta the runner considers progress. Streaming output IS happening (so the idle-read timeout would NOT fire), but the runner is counting wall-clock against the entire request. A single hard problem can blow the 15-minute cap legitimately.\n\nWhy arbitrary timeouts are not helping:\n\na) Same value for cloud upstream-services (claude, codex) and local upstream-services (lmstudio, omlx). Local thinking route-targets routinely need \u003e15 min on hard prompts; claude rarely does. Same cap is wrong for both classes.\n\nb) No way to opt out of the cap per upstream-service, per route-target, per request, per project. /home/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/config.yaml has no knob.\n\nc) Retries inherit the same cap. A timeout-prone session retries with the same fate. Burns cycles and tokens.\n\nd) The cap defeats the cascade: when a cheap-tier qwen times out, escalation to standard claude is intended — but on a thinking route-target the timeout doesn't mean 'broken upstream-service', it means 'this prompt is hard'. We then escalate after wasting 15 min, and the standard tier solves the problem in 2. Net effect of cheap-tier-first becomes negative.","acceptance":"AC1. Upstream-serviceTimeout is configurable per-project via .ddx/config.yaml under agent.routing or agent.endpoints — e.g. agent.endpoints[upstream-service].request_timeout_seconds — and the current 15-minute hardcoded constant becomes a default that can be overridden upward (or removed entirely) per upstream-service.\n\nAC2. Upstream-serviceTimeout is configurable per-tier via the route-target-overrides path: cheap tier on a known thinking route-target (qwen3.6, deepseek-r1, etc.) defaults to a longer cap (e.g. 60 min or unlimited). Cloud chat route-targets keep the 15-min default.\n\nAC3. The idle-read timeout (DefaultUpstream-serviceIdleReadTimeout = 5 min) is the primary stalled-TCP-socket defense; documentation in cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go and the user-facing config docs explain the relationship between idle-read and wall-clock-request timeouts and when each fires.\n\nAC4. When Upstream-serviceTimeout fires, the error message names the exact timeout setting and the path to override it (e.g. 'request_timeout=15m exceeded; configure agent.endpoints.\u003cname\u003e.request_timeout_seconds in .ddx/config.yaml').\n\nAC5. work / execute-bead --request-timeout DURATION CLI flag exists for one-off overrides during debugging.\n\nAC6. Default for known-thinking-route-target surfaces (upstream-service.type=lmstudio AND route-target name matches qwen3.6/qwen-r1/deepseek-r1 etc., or some other declared 'thinking' attribute) is bumped to a sensible value (e.g. 60 min) without requiring per-project config.","labels":["area:agent","area:routing","area:runner","kind:design-flaw"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-2c63bb95","title":"agent: hardcoded 15-minute DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout kills thinking-route-target requests; not configurable","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"Source: cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:30\n  const DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout = 15 * time.Minute\n\nThis is hardcoded, not surfaced through .ddx/config.yaml or any flag, and is applied uniformly to every upstream-service regardless of route-target class. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:99:{"id":"corpus-66a27d2c7bf3","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Remove lossy agent_runner mirror from .ddx/config.yaml and close env-var gap in native config path","description":"Remove DDx's lossy embedded-agent config mirror and close the env-var forwarding gap in the native config path.\n\n## Context\n\nFEAT-006 defines native `.agent/config.yaml` as the authoritative config source for the embedded runner. The current `.ddx/config.yaml` `agent_runner` section is a deprecated mirror that duplicates native runtime config fields. Its precedence semantics are documented in FEAT-006 \"Embedded Runner Configuration Ownership\".\n\n## Goals\n\n- Remove `AgentRunnerConfig` type and `agent_runner` YAML fields from `.ddx/config.yaml` schema once no project uses it\n- Remove `AgentYAMLConfig`, `LLMPresetYAML` mirror types from `cli/internal/agent/runner.go`\n- Remove the `AgentConfigLoader` injection hook and `resolveAgentConfig` fallback path from `agent_runner.go`\n- Apply `AGENT_*` env vars (`AGENT_ROUTE-TARGET`, `AGENT_UPSTREAM-SERVICE`, `AGENT_BASE_URL`, `AGENT_API_KEY`, `AGENT_PRESET`) when resolving from native config, closing the gap documented in `TestConfigPrecedenceEnvVarAppliesInDdxFallbackPath`\n- Wire the `agentRunner()` cmd factory to pass a working-directory path to native config resolution instead of loading the `agent_runner` block\n- Update `TestConfigPrecedenceEnvVarAppliesInDdxFallbackPath` to verify env vars work in the native path after the fix\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n\n- `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` pass with no regressions\n- `.ddx/config.yaml` no longer accepts or uses `agent_runner` fields\n- `AGENT_ROUTE-TARGET` set in the environment overrides the route-target resolved from native `.agent/config.yaml`\n- Existing `.ddx/config.yaml` files with `agent_runner` blocks emit a deprecation warning or are silently ignored (no crash)\n- The migration path in FEAT-006 remains valid","acceptance":"agent_runner mirror removed from DDx config schema; AGENT_* env vars apply in native config path; all tests pass","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:config","area:cli"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-a3b1c8d2","title":"Remove lossy agent_runner mirror from .ddx/config.yaml and close env-var gap in native config path","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Remove DDx's lossy embedded-agent config mirror and close the env-var forwarding gap in the native config path.\n\n## Context\n\nFEAT-006 defines native `.agent/config.yaml` as the authoritative config source for the embedded runner. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:115:{"id":"corpus-7a93d490ad8e","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"bug: embedded agent stalls in post-commit compaction loop","description":"The embedded 'agent' runner can complete a task successfully (commit produced, tests pass) but then fail to terminate, getting stuck in a compaction retry loop with compaction always failing. The parent work never observes task_succeeded and hangs forever.\n\n## Observed\n\nRunning 'ddx agent work --once --local --runner agent --route-target qwen/qwen3-coder-next' against axon-43ed769b (axon auth-core-types bead):\n\n1. qwen3-coder-next ran the task correctly, producing commit 91d4bdb with 636 LOC of types + 109 green tests + clippy clean. The commit was present in the worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-43ed769b-20260415T005652-ebe7a16a.\n\n2. Instead of terminating, the agent kept generating events. After ~20 minutes the session log was 33 MB (8461+ events, 249 messages) and stuck in:\n\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n    ...\n\n3. Parent ddx process in __futex_wait. Had to SIGTERM the parent (PID 4141811) and manually fast-forward-merge the worktree commit to land the work.\n\n4. A1 (previous bead) ran the same runner/route-target and terminated correctly in ~14 min with identical-looking commit shape. The difference was bead size: A2's output was a single 633-line Rust file, vs A1's 350-line SQL + 290-line test file. A2 didn't exceed what A1 did — so the context-limit explanation doesn't fully account for it.\n\n## Expected\n\n- After the agent commits and has nothing more to do, it should emit task_succeeded and terminate\n- Compaction should either succeed (messages_after \u003c messages_before) or bail out cleanly, not retry indefinitely\n- If the context genuinely cannot be compacted, the agent should report a structured failure and preserve the commits, not spin\n\n## Scope\n\n- Detect mid-turn compaction returning success=false repeatedly and bail with a structured 'compaction_stuck' status\n- Bound the total number of compaction attempts in a single turn\n- Ensure task termination happens immediately after a successful commit + no pending tool calls\n- Add a watchdog at the work level: kill the child if no commit+progress event is observed within a budget\n\n## Evidence\n\n- Session log: /Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB)\n- Rescued commit: 91d4bdb on axon master\n- Parent process: PID 4141811 (killed)\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] Compaction retry loop is bounded and fails structurally\n- [ ] Post-commit termination path is deterministic\n- [ ] Watchdog detects and terminates stuck sessions with a preserved-commit outcome\n- [ ] Regression test reproduces the stuck state (seeded session with large no-op tool calls)","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:stability"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-25a41f51","title":"bug: embedded agent stalls in post-commit compaction loop","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"The embedded 'agent' runner can complete a task successfully (commit produced, tests pass) but then fail to terminate, getting stuck in a compaction retry loop with compaction always failing. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:125:{"id":"corpus-84b3a485c4f4","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: preserve unrelated closing provenance when finalizing review beads","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: .ddx/beads.jsonl:178\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit e61d84c36aa74448ecadb2ad9084767cd453bfe0 correctly finalizes review bead hx-9c1a1558, but the same tracker diff also rewrites unrelated closed bead hx-ddx-agent-target-verify and drops its existing closing_commit_sha=2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e. FEAT-004 requires unknown fields to be preserved on read/write operations, and FEAT-019 uses closing_commit_sha as durable replay/audit provenance. Losing that field on an unrelated closed bead silently corrupts the replay baseline for the SD-018 verification task even though this commit did not govern that bead. Suggested fix: repair hx-ddx-agent-target-verify so its closing_commit_sha is restored to 2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e, and add/adjust tracker update coverage so finalizing one bead cannot clobber preserved fields on unrelated rows.","acceptance":"ddx bead show hx-ddx-agent-target-verify --json reports closing_commit_sha=2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e, and regression coverage proves updating or closing hx-9c1a1558 leaves unrelated bead fields unchanged","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:agent","area:cli"],"bead":{"id":"hx-a51dd27a","title":"correctness: preserve unrelated closing provenance when finalizing review beads","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: .ddx/beads.jsonl:178\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit e61d84c36aa74448ecadb2ad9084767cd453bfe0 correctly finalizes review bead hx-9c1a1558, but the same tracker diff also rewrites unrelated closed bead hx-ddx-agent-target-verify and drops its existing closing_commit_sha=2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:126:{"id":"corpus-862310dae8d9","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"integration: persist close provenance in the landed tracker commit","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/bead.go:518\nCategory: integration\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The new close flow writes bead status first, auto-commits .ddx/beads.jsonl, and only then writes closing_commit_sha back to the store. The landed HEAD commit 9494afc82408cac9a650d616fc3207197e1dfbd6 contains only the status=open-\u003eclosed change, while the durable closing_commit_sha appears only in the uncommitted worktree diff. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:130:{"id":"corpus-8da9c3bacad5","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: preserve closing provenance for legitimate .ddx-only work","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/bead.go:80\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit 71f97be suppresses closing_commit_sha whenever the landed close commit touches only paths under .ddx/. FEAT-019 only says to omit metadata-only tracker backfills; dropping provenance for legitimate .ddx-scoped work makes replay/audit fall back to sessionless mode even though the implementation boundary is available.\nSuggested fix: Detect metadata-only close commits more narrowly, for example by distinguishing pure .ddx/beads.jsonl backfills from other tracked .ddx artifacts, and add regression coverage for a close commit that lands tracked .ddx work.","acceptance":"With git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, closing a bead whose landed implementation changes only tracked .ddx/* files still records closing_commit_sha for the landed close commit, and ddx agent replay can use that commit boundary instead of falling back to sessionless/manual mode.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"]}}
./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:134:{"id":"corpus-906830fa2998","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: stop ddx-1210706d replay provenance from pointing at metadata-only commit","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: .ddx/beads.jsonl:1\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit 34b87b9 repoints ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha to 9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88 because that SHA is a pure tracker-only commit. But FEAT-019 replay uses closing_commit_sha as the original commit boundary: it checks out the parent of that SHA and compares against that commit's diff. For ddx-1210706d, that means replay now uses e51936a as the base state but compares against 9653820's one-line metadata diff, so the replay/audit surface no longer represents the actual tracker-governance work that bead governed, including the concerns.md taxonomy change mentioned in the bead acceptance. The last-commit fix removes the mixed-commit problem but replaces it with a metadata-only provenance target that cannot reconstruct the original governed-work diff.\nSuggested fix: Either clear ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha so replay falls back to sessionless/manual semantics for this backfilled bead, or introduce an explicitly documented and tested provenance route-target that distinguishes implementation diff provenance from later tracker-close metadata commits before storing a pure metadata SHA in closing_commit_sha.","acceptance":"ddx bead show ddx-1210706d --json no longer reports closing_commit_sha=9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88, and the provenance field used by FEAT-019 replay is documented or tested to identify the original governed-work diff rather than a later metadata-only tracker commit","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"bead":{"id":"hx-63ac1232","title":"correctness: stop ddx-1210706d replay provenance from pointing at metadata-only commit","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: .ddx/beads.jsonl:1\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit 34b87b9 repoints ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha to 9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88 because that SHA is a pure tracker-only commit. But FEAT-019 replay uses closing_commit_sha as the original commit boundary: it checks out the parent of that SHA and compares against that commit's diff. For ddx-1210706d, that means replay now uses e51936a as the base state but compares against 9653820's one-line metadata diff, so the replay/audit surface no longer represents the actual tracker-governance work that bead governed, including the concerns.md taxonomy change mentioned in the bead acceptance. The last-commit fix removes the mixed-commit problem but replaces it with a metadata-only provenance target that cannot reconstruct the original governed-work diff.\nSuggested fix: Either clear ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha so replay falls back to sessionless/manual semantics for this backfilled bead, or introduce an explicitly documented and tested provenance route-target that distinguishes implementation diff provenance from later tracker-close metadata commits before storing a pure metadata SHA in closing_commit_sha.","acceptance":"ddx bead show ddx-1210706d --json no longer reports closing_commit_sha=9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88, and the provenance field used by FEAT-019 replay is documented or tested to identify the original governed-work diff rather than a later metadata-only tracker commit","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"]}}

Circuit breaker counts consecutive compaction.end(success=false, no_compaction=true) events and aborts with a structured compaction_stuck error after a configurable threshold (default 50).\n\nThe workaround catches the stuck state at the DDx/caller layer. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, run `ddx bead ready --json --execution`.\n2. That behavior is weaker than the API-001 execute-bead supervisor contract, which says the loop should run a generic execution-ready validator against ordered candidates before launch.\n\n## Why this matters\nOpen epics represent umbrella planning/decomposition work, not necessarily a bounded workspace transform. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:211:{"id":"corpus-e3d55d44b044","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Add ddx agent workers command — native live worker status with current bead","description":"There is no CLI command to see which workers are running and which bead each is executing. The WorkerManager in cli/internal/server/workers.go already tracks this (WorkerRecord.CurrentBead at line 51, WorkerRecord.State, WorkerRecord.Runner, WorkerRecord.Route-target, WorkerRecord.Attempts/Successes/Failures) but it is only accessible through the server API. Local --local workers are not tracked by the server at all.\n\nThis bead adds ddx agent workers as a top-level CLI subcommand. It must not depend on worktrunk (wt) or any external tool.\n\n## Required changes\n\n### 1. New command: ddx agent workers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)\nRegister alongside the existing ddx agent log command. Flags:\n  --json         emit raw JSON array\n  --watch        re-render every 2s (like watch -n2) until Ctrl-C\n  --project      filter to a specific project root (default: CWD project)\n\n### 2. Data sources (two paths)\nA. Server-submitted workers: GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/agent/workers — deserialize []WorkerRecord from the server. Already implemented in cli/internal/server/workers.go List() / HTTP handler.\nB. Local --local workers: the server does not track these. Detect them by:\n   1. Reading .ddx/workers/*/status.json files in the project root (server writes these even for local workers when they self-register — confirm in workers.go; if not, fall back to scanning active execute-bead worktrees)\n   2. Scanning .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories (from ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go line 403) and reading the manifest.json inside each to extract bead_id, attempt_id, runner, route-target, started_at\n\n### 3. Local worker detection via manifest scan\nFor each directory matching .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*:\n  - Read .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/manifest.json (path encoded in worktree name suffix)\n  - Check if worktree is still active (git worktree list --porcelain)\n  - Read work-heartbeat-at from bead.Extra (requires ddx-7651506a) if available\n  - Derive synthetic WorkerRecord from manifest fields\n\n### 4. Table output (default)\nColumns: STATE | BEAD | TITLE (truncated 40 chars) | RUNNER | ROUTE-TARGET | ELAPSED | ATTEMPTS\n- STATE: running (green), exited (dim), failed (red)\n- ELAPSED: human-readable (e.g., 4m32s)\n- If current bead title unavailable (local worker pre-manifest), show bead ID\n\n### 5. Merge and deduplicate\nWhen both server and manifest sources are available, deduplicate by worker_id or attempt_id. Server record takes precedence over manifest-derived record for the same worker.\n\n## Key files\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — add newAgentWorkersCommand(), register in newAgentCommand()\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go — WorkerRecord struct (lines 31-54), List API\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant (line 403), manifest shape (lines 726-771)\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry Correlation map (bead_id field)","acceptance":"ddx agent workers prints a table with STATE, BEAD ID, TITLE, RUNNER, ROUTE-TARGET, ELAPSED, ATTEMPTS columns. A worker executing bead axon-abc123 shows that bead ID and its title (truncated). An idle system (no active workers) prints 'no active workers' rather than an empty table. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:231:{"id":"corpus-f744aa57e91b","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Keep execute-bead checkpoint bases free of unrelated DDx runtime churn","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nObserved while running multiple work workers concurrently on 2026-04-11. Replacing git stash with checkpoint commits fixed the immediate beads.lock/workers race, but execute-bead still starts from a dirty repo root where .ddx/beads.jsonl and other DDx-owned execution artifacts may be changing because of other live workers. That means one attempt can inherit unrelated DDx runtime/evidence churn into its checkpoint base.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\nTighten execute-bead checkpointing so an attempt's input snapshot is not polluted by unrelated DDx-generated state from other live executions.\n\n## Scope\n- Define which DDx-owned paths are part of the execution input snapshot vs excluded runtime churn\n- Prevent unrelated live tracker/evidence/worker mutations from being captured into a new attempt base by default\n- Add concurrency-focused tests covering simultaneous workers and dirty DDx-owned state\n- Keep the tracked execution-evidence contract coherent with the new snapshot boundary","acceptance":"Concurrent execute-bead workers no longer checkpoint unrelated DDx runtime churn from other active attempts into their base revisions; the snapshot boundary for DDx-owned files is documented and enforced; and concurrency tests cover the reproduced multi-worker case.","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:correctness","area:agent","area:exec","area:git","area:cli"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-55f05e07","title":"Keep execute-bead checkpoint bases free of unrelated DDx runtime churn","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nObserved while running multiple work workers concurrently on 2026-04-11. Replacing git stash with checkpoint commits fixed the immediate beads.lock/workers race, but execute-bead still starts from a dirty repo root where .ddx/beads.jsonl and other DDx-owned execution artifacts may be changing because of other live workers. That means one attempt can inherit unrelated DDx runtime/evidence churn into its checkpoint base.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\nTighten execute-bead checkpointing so an attempt's input snapshot is not polluted by unrelated DDx-generated state from other live executions.\n\n## Scope\n- Define which DDx-owned paths are part of the execution input snapshot vs excluded runtime churn\n- Prevent unrelated live tracker/evidence/worker mutations from being captured into a new attempt base by default\n- Add concurrency-focused tests covering simultaneous workers and dirty DDx-owned state\n- Keep the tracked execution-evidence contract coherent with the new snapshot boundary","acceptance":"Concurrent execute-bead workers no longer checkpoint unrelated DDx runtime churn from other active attempts into their base revisions; the snapshot boundary for DDx-owned files is documented and enforced; and concurrency tests cover the reproduced multi-worker case.","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:correctness","area:agent","area:exec","area:git","area:cli"],"parent":"ddx-32b3008e"}}
./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:238:{"id":"corpus-011259e83fe4","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"decomposable","rationale":"repeated reviewer blocks cite decomposition scope","title":"workersByProject compares project id to project path: per-project workers view always empty","description":"## Observed\n\nUser clicks \"Drain queue\" on the project home page. Mutation returns success with a worker id. Navigating to `/nodes/.../projects/\u003cid\u003e/workers` shows no workers — an empty table.\n\nVerified on disk: the worker record exists and is running.\n\n```\n$ cat .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T205846-f23f/status.json\n{\n  \"id\": \"worker-20260422T205846-f23f\",\n  \"kind\": \"work\",\n  \"state\": \"running\",\n  \"project_root\": \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx\",\n  ...\n}\n```\n\nSo the dispatch worked, but the per-project workers list filters it out.\n\n## Root cause\n\n`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:156` in `GetWorkersGraphQL`:\n\n```go\nif projectID != \"\" \u0026\u0026 rec.ProjectRoot != projectID {\n    continue\n}\n```\n\n- `projectID` is the GraphQL argument — a project **id** like `proj-96d7ea83`.\n- `rec.ProjectRoot` is the worker record field — a project **path** like `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx`.\n\nThese are different representations and never match. With any non-empty `projectID`, every worker is filtered out.\n\nThe global `workers` resolver (`resolver_agent.go:9`) passes `\"\"` and works correctly. Only the per-project path (`workersByProject` → `GetWorkersGraphQL(projectID)`) is broken. The user's flow (dispatch from project home → navigate to project workers) hits exactly this path.\n\nNote: `GetBeadSnapshots` (state_graphql.go:49-105) handles the same concept correctly by iterating `s.Projects` and filtering on `proj.ID != projectID` — comparing id to id. The worker resolver does not use that pattern.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nTwo options; prefer (1) for minimal change, (2) for long-term clarity.\n\n### Option 1 — Resolve id → path at the filter site\n\n```go\nexpectedPath := \"\"\nif projectID != \"\" {\n    proj, ok := s.GetProjectByID(projectID)\n    if !ok {\n        return nil // unknown project id → no workers\n    }\n    expectedPath = proj.Path\n}\n...\nif expectedPath != \"\" \u0026\u0026 rec.ProjectRoot != expectedPath {\n    continue\n}\n```\n\n### Option 2 — Store project id on the worker record\n\nAdd `ProjectID string` to `WorkerRecord`. Populate at creation time in `WorkerManager.StartExecuteLoop` via `s.GetProjectByPath(effectiveRoot)`. Filter on `rec.ProjectID != projectID`. Cleaner — the worker record carries its own identity and the query doesn't depend on runtime resolution. Requires a tiny migration (for older records missing the field, fall back to path-resolve as in Option 1).\n\nRecommend Option 2. It removes the representation mismatch for good and is cheap.\n\n## Scope note\n\nThis is a one-line functional bug with outsized impact: it's the reason the Workers page looks broken whenever used as the dashboard intends. Prioritizing P1.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Workers pane lifecycle controls (`ddx-69789664`).\n- Cross-project workers view — the global `workers` query already handles that.","acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, clicking Drain queue on a project home page starts a worker that I can immediately see in that project's Workers view, with its state and current bead updating live.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Root-cause fix.** The `workersByProject(projectID:)` resolver returns all workers whose target project matches the requested id. Both Option 1 (id→path resolution) and Option 2 (store project id on record) satisfy this; implementation chooses one with a one-line justification in the PR description.\n\n2. **Regression test — project-scoped query.**\n   - Seed a ServerState with two registered projects (different paths and ids).\n   - Start one worker for each project.\n   - `workersByProject(projectID: \u003cid-1\u003e)` returns exactly worker 1.\n   - `workersByProject(projectID: \u003cid-2\u003e)` returns exactly worker 2.\n   - `workers` (global) returns both.\n   - Unknown project id returns empty list, not an error.\n\n3. **Regression test — backward compat.**\n   - A worker record on disk without a `ProjectID` field (simulating a pre-migration record) is correctly matched by the path-resolution fallback. Only required if Option 2 is chosen.\n\n4. **End-to-end validation.**\n   - Playwright: navigate to project home, click Drain, confirm, wait 2s, navigate to Workers tab. Assert exactly one new row with `state: running` and matching worker id.\n   - Same test run against a second project asserts that project's Workers tab does not show project-1's worker.\n\n5. **No regression to unrelated surfaces.**\n   - Global `workers` query still returns all workers.\n   - Worker detail page still loads by worker id.\n   - Workers live-progress subscription still patches rows in place.\n\n6. **Cross-reference.**\n   - Note on `ddx-69789664` (Workers lifecycle controls): that bead's acceptance assumed Workers pane worked correctly. Point to this bead as the prerequisite for those tests to be meaningful.","labels":["feat-008","feat-010","bug","graphql"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-05b4cc9d","title":"workersByProject compares project id to project path: per-project workers view always empty","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"## Observed\n\nUser clicks \"Drain queue\" on the project home page. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:239:{"id":"corpus-078aec002664","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"decomposable","rationale":"no_changes/review text says bead needs splitting","title":"test: migrate TestExecuteBeadNoMerge to real git + script runner","description":"Migrate cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go TestExecuteBeadNoMerge() from fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner to real git + script runner.\n\nPattern:\n- Use cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go newScriptRunnerRepo(t, beadCount) to build an isolated repo with real git.\n- Use runGitInteg (from cli/internal/git/git_basic_test.go) for any supplemental git invocations, so GIT_* env is scrubbed.\n- Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit with the RealLandingGitOps flow used in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go.\n- Replace fakeAgentRunner with the script runner (cli/internal/agent/script.go) driven by a per-attempt directive file. When the test needs to simulate 'agent emits no commits' or 'agent emits a dirty worktree', encode that as script directives rather than a canned Result struct.\n- Preserve every assertion — the point is that the test now exercises real git + real merge/preserve semantics, not that the surface shrinks.\n\nScope limit: ONLY this test. No struct ending in -GitOps or -Runner that fakes the thing under test is referenced from this test after the migration.\n\nGoverning concern: concerns.md §testing 'no mocks, period' + 'never mock the thing you are testing'. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:247:{"id":"corpus-5bf13c883cad","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"decomposable","rationale":"three or more attempts without a closing commit","title":"Detect execute-bead worktree edits even when the agent makes no commits","description":"A Codex work run on ddx-28c15f0e updated FEAT-002, SD-019, and website server docs inside the managed worktree, but DDx classified the attempt as no_changes because it only looked for agent-created commits. Execute-bead must treat a dirty worktree with tracked edits as real output, preserve or land it appropriately, and record accurate result status instead of discarding useful work.","acceptance":"execute-bead and work no longer report no_changes when the worktree contains tracked file modifications without agent-created commits; the attempt preserves or lands the changes and result artifacts reflect the actual diff state","labels":["helix","area:exec","area:git","area:agent","kind:correctness"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-45f6d738","title":"Detect execute-bead worktree edits even when the agent makes no commits","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"A Codex work run on ddx-28c15f0e updated FEAT-002, SD-019, and website server docs inside the managed worktree, but DDx classified the attempt as no_changes because it only looked for agent-created commits. Execute-bead must treat a dirty worktree with tracked edits as real output, preserve or land it appropriately, and record accurate result status instead of discarding useful work.","acceptance":"execute-bead and work no longer report no_changes when the worktree contains tracked file modifications without agent-created commits; the attempt preserves or lands the changes and result artifacts reflect the actual diff state","labels":["helix","area:exec","area:git","area:agent","kind:correctness"]}}

./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:259:{"id":"corpus-ef0f9a2702f5","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"decomposable","rationale":"no_changes/review text says bead needs splitting","title":"execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence","description":"Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. Result: iteration commit is built by ddx-land-coordinator with ONLY execution-evidence files (.ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/result.json, manifest.json, usage.json), no actual code or doc changes. The post-merge codex reviewer correctly flags BLOCK with rationale 'change set only adds execution metadata files'.\n\nConcrete evidence (axon repo, 2026-04-29):\n\n  Attempt: 20260429T235353-d14f6b91 (axon-80979cb8 / A1 sub-bead)\n  Runner: claude / route-target: sonnet\n  Duration: 817 sec, cost $3.61, 132 tool calls\n  Tool breakdown: 79 Bash, 34 Read, 11 Grep, 8 Edit\n  Edit targets: ui/src/lib/api.ts, ui/src/lib/policy-evaluator.ts,\n    ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte, ui/tests/e2e/link-policy-retry.spec.ts\n  Iteration commit (ddx-land-coordinator): contains exactly 2 files —\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/result.json (23 lines)\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/usage.json (9 lines)\n  Net: zero actual edits landed despite $3.61 of route-target time spent making them.\n\nThe same pattern appeared on:\n  20260429T235406-20eeaf2c (axon-db7a6d0a, claude/sonnet, $0.57, 4.2 min, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190657-fa91f49b (axon-ab2e52e0, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.08, 24s, stalled)\n  20260429T190634-579df880 (axon-80979cb8, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.12, 104s, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190646-5aa5b46e (axon-db7a6d0a, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.06, 25s, BLOCK)\n\nTotal burn today: ~$4.50 across 5 attempts on 3 beads, 0 closed.\n\nThe execute-bead prompt template tells the agent to commit:\n  '4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with git add \u003cspecific-paths\u003e...'\n\nSo agent self-commit is the contract. But:\n\n(a) When typecheck/lint/test fails, the agent is told to fix or write no_changes_rationale.txt — but apparently many agents are bailing silently without writing the rationale either, so the runner sees a clean worktree (no diff staged or committed) and treats it as 'agent made no commits'. The iteration then carries only the auto-generated execution evidence.\n\n(b) There may also be a path where the agent commits but a pre-commit hook silently rejects it. Have not confirmed; the worktree was reaped before I could inspect.\n\nThe issue spans multiple runneres (claude, codex via embedded openrouter), so it is not specific to one upstream-service's commit semantics. Either:\n  - The runner should reject 'agent made no commits AND no no_changes_rationale.txt' as a separate failure class instead of silently letting ddx-land-coordinator land an empty iteration\n  - The agent prompt should make rationale-writing on incomplete work non-optional with stronger language\n  - The runner should run a final-stage check 'did the agent produce ANY signal — commit OR rationale — and if not, treat as runner-execution-failed and surface to the operator'","acceptance":"AC1. When an execute-bead session exits without committing in the worktree AND without writing .ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/no_changes_rationale.txt, the runner records a NEW dedicated status (e.g. 'agent_silent_exit' or 'no_evidence_produced') instead of letting ddx-land-coordinator land an iteration containing only execution evidence.\n\nAC2. The codex reviewer's BLOCK rationale ('change set only adds execution metadata files') becomes redundant — the runner catches this case before the reviewer is even invoked, saving a redundant review pass and its cost.\n\nAC3. The execute-bead prompt template is updated so the rationale-on-incomplete-work instruction is harder to skip — ideally a tool the agent must invoke (e.g. SubmitNoChangesRationale tool) rather than 'write a file and exit'.\n\nAC4. When the agent did run Edit tool calls but the worktree has no commit, the runner logs a warning naming each file the agent edited that is now uncommitted, so operators can recover the work or diagnose the silent-commit-failure.\n\nAC5. Documentation in docs/ explains the agent-self-commit contract, the rationale-file fallback, and the new 'agent_silent_exit' classification.","labels":["area:agent","area:runner","area:execute-bead","kind:correctness"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-4e0a4a59","title":"execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)\n- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)\n- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path\n- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end\n- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server\n- [ ] cargo test --workspace green\n- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.\n\n## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)\n\n- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug\n- GraphQL under the nested path\n- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404\n- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths\n- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST\n\n## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)\n\n### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced\n\nB1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.\n\nWire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.\n\nCreate the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.\n\nThe legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.\n\n### 2. Audit attribution not populated\n\nF1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.\n\nFind every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(\u0026request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.\n\n### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database\n\n`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: \u0026str = \"x-axon-database\"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:\n\n(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR\n(b) A new field on every request proto\n\n(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `\"{tenant}:{database}\"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.\n\nAlso add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\n\nFor this bead, the gRPC change is small:\n- Rename the metadata constant\n- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form\n- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form\n- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\n\n### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)\n\nD2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.\n\n## Scope summary\n\nThis bead does exactly three things:\n\n1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)\n2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present\n3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata\n\n## Tests for this bead\n\n### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)\n\n- create a JwtIssuer with a known key\n- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)\n- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses\n- send a request with Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections\n- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)\n\n### Integration test: attribution populated\n\n- send an entity-create request with a JWT\n- query the audit log\n- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub\n\n### gRPC test: composite slug\n\n- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`\n- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)\n\n### Manual smoke test\n\nStart the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred\n- OIDC federation — follow-up\n- Signing key rotation — future ADR\n- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths\n\n## Files to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)\n- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup\n- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path\n- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW\n- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal\n- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes\n- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes\n\n## Verification\n\n- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)\n- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh runs end-to-end against a dev Integration tests for the JWT auth middleware (ADR-018 §4).\\n//!\\n//! Builds a minimal axum router with a single protected data-plane route:\\n//!   GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\n//!\\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\\n\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::body::Body;\\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\\nuse axum::routing::get;\\nuse axum::Router;\\nuse http::Request;\\nuse serde_json::Value;\\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{\\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\\n};\\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\\n\\nconst ISSUER: \u0026str = \\\"integration-issuer\\\";\\nconst TENANT: \u0026str = \\\"acme\\\";\\nconst DATABASE: \u0026str = \\\"orders\\\";\\nconst USER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"u-integration-01\\\";\\nconst SECRET: \u0026[u8] = b\\\"integration-test-secret\\\";\\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: \u0026str = \\\"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\\";\\n\\nfn now_secs() -\u003e u64 {\\n    SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs()\\n}\\n\\nfn make_state() -\u003e Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e {\\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\\n    storage.insert_user(User {\\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        display_name: \\\"Integration User\\\".to_string(),\\n        email: None,\\n        created_at_ms: 0,\\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\\n    });\\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\\n            Box::new(storage) as Box\u003cdyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\\n        )),\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nfn make_app(state: Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e) -\u003e Router {\\n    Router::new()\\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n            state,\\n            jwt_verify_layer,\\n        ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, Value) {\\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\\\"service should not fail\\\");\\n    let status = resp.status();\\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\\n        .await\\n        .unwrap_or_default();\\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(\u0026bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\\n    (status, json)\\n}\\n\\nfn bearer(token: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn valid_token(issuer: \u0026JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -\u003e String {\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants,\\n    };\\n    issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn orders_read_grants() -\u003e Grants {\\n    Grants {\\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\\n        }],\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated ──────────────────────────────\\n\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\\n    assert_eq!(\\n        json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\"),\\n        \\\"unauthenticated\\\"\\n    );\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with read grants → 200 ──────────────────────────────────\\n\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn integration_valid_jwt_with_read_grant_returns_200() {\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let token = valid_token(\u0026state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n        .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", bearer(\u0026token))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\\n\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: \\\"different-tenant\\\".to_string(), // URL says \\\"acme\\\"\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\\n    };\\n    let token = state.issuer.issue(\u0026claims).unwrap();\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n        .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", bearer(\u0026token))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\\n    assert_eq!(\\n        json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\"),\\n        \\\"credential_wrong_tenant\\\"\\n    );\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 database_not_granted ──────────\\n\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn integration_wrong_database_returns_403() {\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let grants = Grants {\\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n            name: \\\"invoices\\\".to_string(), // URL says \\\"orders\\\"\\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\\n        }],\\n    };\\n    let token = valid_token(\u0026state.issuer, grants);\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n        .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", bearer(\u0026token))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\\n    assert_eq!(\\n        json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\"),\\n        \\\"database_not_granted\\\"\\n GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\n//!\\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\\n\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::body::Body;\\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\\nuse axum::routing::get;\\nuse axum::Router;\\nuse http::Request;\\nuse serde_json::Value;\\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{\\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\\n};\\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\\n\\nconst ISSUER: \u0026str = \\\"integration-issuer\\\";\\nconst TENANT: \u0026str = \\\"acme\\\";\\nconst DATABASE: \u0026str = \\\"orders\\\";\\nconst USER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"u-integration-01\\\";\\nconst SECRET: \u0026[u8] = b\\\"integration-test-secret\\\";\\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: \u0026str = \\\"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\\";\\n\\nfn now_secs() -\u003e u64 {\\n    SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs()\\n}\\n\\nfn make_state() -\u003e Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e {\\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\\n    storage.insert_user(User {\\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        display_name: \\\"Integration User\\\".to_string(),\\n        email: None,\\n        created_at_ms: 0,\\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\\n    });\\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\\n            Box::new(storage) as Box\u003cdyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\\n        )),\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nfn make_app(state: Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e) -\u003e Router {\\n    Router::new()\\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n            state,\\n            jwt_verify_layer,\\n        ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, Value) {\\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\\\"service should not fail\\\");\\n    let status = resp.status();\\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\\n        .await\\n        .unwrap_or_default();\\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(\u0026bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\\n    (status, json)\\n}\\n\\nfn bearer(token: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn valid_token(issuer: \u0026JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -\u003e String {\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants,\\n    };\\n    issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn orders_read_grants() -\u003e Grants {\\n    Grants {\\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\\n        }],\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated ──────────────────────────────\\n\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\\n    assert_eq!(\\n        json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\"),\\n        \\\"unauthenticated\\\"\\n    );\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with read grants → 200 ──────────────────────────────────\\n\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn integration_valid_jwt_with_read_grant_returns_200() {\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let token = valid_token(\u0026state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n        .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", bearer(\u0026token))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\\n\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: \\\"different-tenant\\\".to_string(), // URL says \\\"acme\\\"\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\\n    };\\n    let token = state.issuer.issue(\u0026claims).unwrap();\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n        .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", bearer(\u0026token))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\\n    assert_eq!(\\n        json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\"),\\n        \\\"credential_wrong_tenant\\\"\\n    );\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why.
