JWT-based auth pipeline: sign, verify, and enforce credentials (ADR-018 §4).\\n//!\\n//! # Verification order\\n//!\\n//! 1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` → `Unauthenticated` if missing.\\n//! 2. Parse + verify HS256 signature → `CredentialInvalid` / `CredentialMalformed`.\\n//! 3. Check `iss` against issuer ID → `CredentialForeignIssuer`.\\n//! 4. Check `exp`/`nbf` with ±30 s skew → `CredentialExpired` / `CredentialNotYetValid`.\\n//! 5. Check `jti` revocation (cache → storage) → `CredentialRevoked`.\\n//! 6. Compare `aud` to URL tenant → `CredentialWrongTenant`.\\n//! 7. Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\\n//! 9. Walk grants for URL database + HTTP-method-derived op → `DatabaseNotGranted` / `OpNotGranted`.\\n//!\\n//! On success, installs `ResolvedIdentity` into the request extension.\\n//! On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\\n\\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::extract::State;\\nuse axum::middleware::Next;\\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\\nuse http::{HeaderMap, Method, StatusCode};\\nuse jsonwebtoken::{Algorithm, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, Validation, decode, encode};\\nuse serde_json::json;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{AuthError, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, UserId};\\nuse axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\\n\\n// ── JwtIssuer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\\npub struct JwtIssuer {\\n    secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e,\\n    /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\\n    pub issuer_id: String,\\n}\\n\\nimpl JwtIssuer {\\n    /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e, issuer_id: String) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\\n    pub fn issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e {\\n        encode(\\n            \u0026Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\\n            claims,\\n            \u0026EncodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret),\\n        )\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\\n    ///\\n    /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\\n    /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\\n    pub fn verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e {\\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\\n        validation.validate_exp = false;\\n        validation.validate_nbf = false;\\n        validation.validate_aud = false;\\n        validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\\n\\n        decode::\u003cJwtClaims\u003e(token, \u0026DecodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret), \u0026validation)\\n            .map(|td| td.claims)\\n            .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature =\u003e AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                _ =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n            })\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── InMemoryRevocationCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\\n///\\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\\n#[derive(Default)]\\npub struct InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    inner: RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    /// Create an empty cache.\\n    pub fn new() -\u003e Self {\\n        Self::default()\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\\n    pub fn contains(\u0026self, jti: \u0026Uuid) -\u003e bool {\\n        self.inner\\n            .read()\\n            .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\\n            .unwrap_or(false)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Add `jti` to the cache.\\n    pub fn insert(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) {\\n        if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\\n            g.insert(jti);\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthPipelineState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\\n///\\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to `axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state`.\\n#[derive(Clone)]\\npub struct AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Token issuer / verifier.\\n    pub issuer: Arc\u003cJwtIssuer\u003e,\\n    /// Fast in-process revocation check.\\n    pub revocation_cache: Arc\u003cInMemoryRevocationCache\u003e,\\n    /// Authoritative storage for user, membership, and revocation data.\\n    pub storage: Arc\u003cMutex\u003cBox\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Convenience constructor.\\n    pub fn new(issuer: JwtIssuer, storage: Box\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self {\\n            issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n            revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n            storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Middleware ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Axum middleware that enforces the ADR-018 §4 JWT verification order.\\n///\\n/// Install with:\\n/// ```ignore\\n/// Router::new()\\n///     .route(\\\"/tenants/:t/databases/:db/…\\\", …)\\n///     .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n///         Arc::new(state),\\n///         jwt_verify_layer,\\n///     ))\\n/// ```\\npub async fn jwt_verify_layer(\\n    State(state): State\u003cArc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e\u003e,\\n    request: axum::extract::Request,\\n    next: Next,\\n) -\u003e Response {\\n    let path = request.uri().path().to_string();\\n\\n    // Skip middleware for paths that are not data-plane paths.\\n    let Some((tenant, database)) = extract_tenant_database(\u0026path) else {\\n        return next.run(request).await;\\n    };\\n\\n    let tenant = tenant.to_string();\\n    let database = database.to_string();\\n    let method = request.method().clone();\\n    let headers = request.headers().clone();\\n\\n    match verify_request(\u0026state, \u0026headers, \u0026method, \u0026tenant, \u0026database) {\\n        Ok(identity) =\u003e {\\n            let mut request = request;\\n            request.extensions_mut().insert(identity);\\n            next.run(request).await\\n        }\\n        Err(err) =\u003e AuthErrorResponse(err).into_response(),\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Core verification logic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfn verify_request(\\n    state: \u0026AuthPipelineState,\\n    headers: \u0026HeaderMap,\\n    method: \u0026Method,\\n    tenant: \u0026str,\\n    database: \u0026str,\\n) -\u003e Result\u003cResolvedIdentity, AuthError\u003e {\\n    // Step 1: Extract Bearer token.\\n    let token = extract_bearer_token(headers)?;\\n\\n    // Step 2: Verify JWT signature and structure.\\n    let claims = state.issuer.verify(token)?;\\n\\n    // Step 3: Check iss.\\n    if claims.iss != state.issuer.issuer_id {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 4: Check exp/nbf with 30 s skew.\\n    let now = SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs();\\n    const SKEW: u64 = 30;\\n    // Expired: exp + skew \u003c now  ↔  expired more than `skew` seconds ago\\n    if claims.exp.saturating_add(SKEW) \u003c now {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialExpired);\\n    }\\n    // Not yet valid: nbf \u003e now + skew  ↔  valid more than `skew` seconds from now\\n    if claims.nbf \u003e now.saturating_add(SKEW) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 5: Check JTI revocation (cache first, then storage).\\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims\\n        .jti\\n        .parse()\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n    if state.revocation_cache.contains(\u0026jti_uuid) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n    }\\n    {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        if storage\\n            .is_jti_revoked(jti_uuid)\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?\\n        {\\n            // Populate the cache so subsequent checks avoid another storage round-trip.\\n            drop(storage);\\n            state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\\n            return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 6: Compare aud to URL tenant.\\n    if claims.aud != tenant {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 7: Look up user, check suspension.\\n    let user_id = UserId::new(\u0026claims.sub);\\n    let user = {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        storage\\n            .get_user(user_id.clone())\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n            .ok_or(AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n    };\\n    if user.suspended_at_ms.is_some() {\\n        return Err(AuthError::UserSuspended);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 8: Check tenant membership.\\n    let tenant_id = TenantId::new(tenant);\\n    {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        storage\\n            .get_tenant_member(tenant_id.clone(), user_id.clone())\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::NotATenantMember)?\\n            .ok_or(AuthError::NotATenantMember)?;\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 9: Walk grants for the URL database + HTTP-method-derived op.\\n    let granted_db = claims\\n        .grants\\n        .find_database(database)\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted)?;\\n\\n    let required_op = if method == Method::GET {\\n        Op::Read\\n    } else {\\n        Op::Write\\n    };\\n    if !granted_db.has_op(required_op) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::OpNotGranted);\\n    }\\n\\n    Ok(ResolvedIdentity {\\n        user_id,\\n        tenant_id,\\n        grants: claims.grants,\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Extract the `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` value from the `Authorization` header.\\nfn extract_bearer_token(headers: \u0026HeaderMap) -\u003e Result\u003c\u0026str, AuthError\u003e {\\n    let auth_header = headers\\n        .get(http::header::AUTHORIZATION)\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n\\n    let auth_str = auth_header\\n        .to_str()\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n\\n    auth_str\\n        .strip_prefix(\\\"Bearer \\\")\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n}\\n\\n/// Extract `(tenant, database)` from a data-plane URL path.\\n///\\n/// Returns `Some((tenant, database))` for paths matching\\n/// `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/…`, `None` otherwise.\\n/// Non-data-plane paths skip the middleware entirely.\\npub fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e {\\n    let path = path.strip_prefix('/')?;\\n    let mut parts = path.splitn(5, '/');\\n    match (parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next()) {\\n        (Some(\\\"tenants\\\"), Some(tenant), Some(\\\"databases\\\"), Some(rest))\\n            if !tenant.is_empty() \u0026\u0026 !rest.is_empty() =\u003e\\n        {\\n            let database = rest.split('/').next()?;\\n            if database.is_empty() {\\n                None\\n            } else {\\n                Some((tenant, database))\\n            }\\n        }\\n        _ =\u003e None,\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthError → axum Response ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Newtype wrapper that lets us implement `IntoResponse` for `AuthError` in\\n/// this crate (orphan-rule: neither `IntoResponse` nor `AuthError` is local).\\npub struct AuthErrorResponse(pub .unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\\n        let body = json!({\\n            \\\"error\\\": {\\n                \\\"code\\\": self.0.error_code(),\\n                \\\"message\\\": self.0.to_string()\\n            }\\n        });\\n        (status, axum::Json(body)).into_response()\\n TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants: Grants {\\n            databases: Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n            state,\\n            jwt_verify_layer,\\n        ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. // TestPreCommitDDXValidateFailsOnCoreWorktreeRedirect verifies the pre-commit
// guard used by lefthook fails when local config points the primary checkout
// at a different worktree.
func TestPreCommitDDXValidateFailsOnCoreWorktreeRedirect(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	initTestRepo(t, dir)

	redirect := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "redirected-worktree")
	add := exec.Command("git", "worktree", "add", "--detach", redirect, "HEAD")
	add.Dir = dir
	add.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
	require.NoError(t, add.Run())

	set := exec.Command("git", "config", "core.worktree", redirect)
	set.Dir = dir
	set.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
	require.NoError(t, set.Run())

	script, absErr := filepath.Abs(filepath.Join("..", "..", "scripts", "git-config-health.sh"))
	require.NoError(t, absErr)
	cmd := exec.Command("sh", script)
	cmd.Dir = dir
	cmd.Env = append(gitpkg.CleanEnv(), "DDX_GIT_CONFIG_HEALTH_ROOT="+dir)
	out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
	require.Error(t, err, "hook guard should fail on redirected core.worktree")
	assert.Contains(t, string(out), "Invalid local git config: core.worktree=")
	assert.Contains(t, string(out), "git config --unset core.worktree")

	lefthook, readErr := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join("..", "..", "lefthook.yml"))
	require.NoError(t, readErr)
	assert.Contains(t, string(lefthook), "git-config-health:")
	assert.Contains(t, string(lefthook), "sh scripts/git-config-health.sh")
}

// TestPreCommitGitConfigHealthFailsOnCoreHooksPath verifies the pre-commit
// guard fails before lefthook can silently continue with stale hook-path config.
func TestPreCommitGitConfigHealthFailsOnCoreHooksPath(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	initTestRepo(t, dir)

	set := exec.Command("git", "config", "core.hooksPath", ".git/hooks")
	set.Dir = dir
	set.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
	require.NoError(t, set.Run())

	script, absErr := filepath.Abs(filepath.Join("..", "..", "scripts", "git-config-health.sh"))
	require.NoError(t, absErr)
	cmd := exec.Command("sh", script)
	cmd.Dir = dir
	cmd.Env = append(gitpkg.CleanEnv(), "DDX_GIT_CONFIG_HEALTH_ROOT="+dir)
	out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
	require.Error(t, err, "hook guard should fail on local core.hooksPath")
	assert.Contains(t, string(out), "Invalid local git config: core.hooksPath=.git/hooks")
	assert.Contains(t, string(out), "git config --unset-all --local core.hooksPath")
}

// TestCheckGitRepoHealthCleanRepo verifies a clean repo produces no corruption
// issues (only the optional worktreeConfig warning at most).
func TestCheckGitRepoHealthCleanRepo(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	initTestRepo(t, dir)

	issues := checkGitRepoHealth(dir, false)

	assert.False(t, hasIssueType(issues, "git_core_bare_corruption"),
		"clean repo should not report core.bare corruption")
	assert.False(t, hasIssueType(issues, "git_stray_core_worktree"),
		"clean repo should not report stray core.worktree")
}

// TestCheckGitRepoHealthWorktreeConfigWarning verifies that a missing
// extensions.worktreeConfig surfaces as a (non-fatal) warning issue.
func TestCheckGitRepoHealthWorktreeConfigWarning(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	initTestRepo(t, dir)

	issues := checkGitRepoHealth(dir, false)
	assert.True(t, hasIssueType(issues, "git_worktree_config_disabled"),
		"expected git_worktree_config_disabled warning on fresh repo")

	// Enable it, then the warning should disappear.
	en := exec.Command("git", "config", "extensions.worktreeConfig", "true")
	en.Dir = dir
	en.Env = gitpkg.CleanEnv()
	require.NoError(t, en.Run())

	issues = checkGitRepoHealth(dir, false)
	assert.False(t, hasIssueType(issues, "git_worktree_config_disabled"),
		"warning should clear once extensions.worktreeConfig=true")
}

data.text: The static-audit AC turned out to be materially larger than the nominal slice: the exact grep still matches hundreds of `.ddx` path joins across production code and tests, spread across many packages. That crosses the execute-bead size gate, so I’m switching to a decomposition pass and will file narrower child beads instead of making partial code changes.

ID:       ddx-e4c3d791
Title:    ddx home: presence-based root resolution + per-project worktree registry: part 4
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 0
Labels:   area:config, area:refactor, kind:refactor, phase:3, initiative:zero-footprint, principle:ddx-home, decomposed
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-06cbaa90
Desc:     PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-06cbaa90 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 4 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/serv
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestWorktreeRegistry_SubsequentRegisterNonMaster asserts later worktrees register but do not overwrite master.
- TestWorktreeRegistry_OperatorRePin asserts `ddx project worktree master <path>` updates the pin.
- Static check via lefthook: `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits. The lint rule is wired into pre-commit.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
Accept:   1. TestWorktreeRegistry_SubsequentRegisterNonMaster asserts later worktrees register but do not overwrite master.
2. TestWorktreeRegistry_OperatorRePin asserts `ddx project worktree master <path>` updates the pin.
3. Static check via lefthook: `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits. The lint rule is wired into pre-commit.

# Bead Authoring Template

> A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent
> sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation.
> — Reliability principle **P7** (`docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md`)

This document is the canonical template + rubric for creating beads in
this repository. It exists because the 2026-05-03 bead-quality audit
(`.ddx/executions/20260503T155638-bead57f0cb9e/bead-quality-audit-2026-05-03.md`)
found that only **1 of 20** representative open beads scored 8/8 on
the sufficient-sub-agent-prompt rubric. The most common failure modes
were: no specific test names in AC (80 % miss), no `go test` +
lefthook gate in AC (80 % miss), and no root cause with file:line in
description (65 % miss).

---

## 1. The 8-criterion rubric

Every bead must satisfy:

| # | Criterion | Verifier |
|---|-----------|----------|
| (a) | **Title**: one-line scope clarity, imperative, names subsystem + change | "Pagination off-by-one in bead list endpoint" not "Pagination bug" |
| (b) | **Description**: problem + ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE + proposed fix + non-scope | Root cause cites `cli/path/file.go:42-50` |
| (c) | **AC**: numbered, verifiable, includes specific test names | At least one AC names `TestFoo` or a `go test -run` filter |
| (d) | **AC** includes "wired-in" assertion when introducing new code paths | New function → AC asserts a caller invokes it (production or test) |
| (e) | **AC** includes `go test` command + lefthook gate | Both `cd cli && go test ./...` and `lefthook run pre-commit` named |
| (f) | **Labels**: phase + area + kind + cross-refs | `phase:N`, `area:*`, `kind:*`, plus adr/spec/prevention if applicable |
| (g) | **Parent + Deps**: explicit | Parent set; deps either listed or "no deps" stated |
| (h) | Description reads as **sufficient sub-agent prompt** | A competent agent given only the bead body can pick a file and run tests without asking |

Score 8/8 = execution-ready. Less = retrofit before dispatch.

---

## 2. Template fields

```text
TITLE
  <area>: <imperative one-line scope>
  e.g. "agent: unify ExecuteLoopSpec across cobra/HTTP/server layers"

TYPE
  task | bug | epic | chore

LABELS
  phase:<N>, area:<subsystem>, kind:<category>
  + cross-refs: prevention, adr:<id>, spec:<id>, story:<n>, refactor

PARENT
  <ddx-id> of governing epic / story / spec
  (omit only if this is a root)

DEPS
  - <ddx-id-1>: <one-line why this dep matters>
  - <ddx-id-2>: <one-line why this dep matters>
  (or explicit "No deps.")

SPEC-ID (when applicable)
  --set spec-id=FEAT-NNN  | SD-NNN | TD-NNN | ADR-NNN

DESCRIPTION
  PROBLEM
    <one-paragraph statement of what is broken or missing>

  ROOT CAUSE
    <function/file:line where the defect lives>
    <what the code does today vs what it should do>
    For new features: ROOT CAUSE -> "no existing implementation"
    is OK; replace with CURRENT STATE describing the gap.

  PROPOSED FIX
    <bulleted concrete changes; cite file:line for each>
    - cli/internal/foo/bar.go:120-145  rename Baz -> Quux
    - cli/internal/foo/bar_test.go      add TestQuux_RoundTrip

  NON-SCOPE
    - <thing readers might assume is included but isn't>
    - <thing covered by sister bead ddx-NNNN>

  INTERSECTIONS (optional)
    - <ddx-id>: <how this bead relates to that one>

ACCEPTANCE
  1. <verifiable assertion 1 — names a Test, file path, command, or
     observable artifact>
  2. <verifiable assertion 2>
  3. ...
  N-1. cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green.
  N. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
```

---

## 3. Examples

### 3.1 Example of a strong bead (audit score 8/8)

`ddx-1e516bc9` — *"fizeau Execute: explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower must route within harness's eligible models"*

Why it scored 8/8:

- **Title (a)** names subsystem (`fizeau Execute`), the precondition (`explicit Harness + empty Model`), and the expected behavior (`route within harness's eligible models`).
- **Description (b)** opens with **OBSERVED** (one paragraph, file:line) → **SCOPE — UPSTREAM (fizeau) ONLY** → **INVESTIGATION FIRST** (with explicit close-as-no-op outcome) → **UPSTREAM CHANGE** (file:line into `service_execute.go:193-224`, `models.yaml:31-60`, `registry.go`) → explicit **NOT IN SCOPE** list naming five excluded paths.
- **AC (c)** names two concrete tests:
  `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelWithProfile_RoutesWithinHarness` and
  `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelNoProfile_FailsClearly`.
- **Wired-in (d)**: AC #6 asserts the resolved model surfaces in
  `.ddx/workers/<id>/spec.json`, `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json`, and the
  workers UI — three distinct call-graph endpoints.
- **Test command + lefthook (e)**: AC #9 — `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.`
- **Labels (f)**: `phase:2, area:agent, kind:fix, upstream-fizeau, observed-failure`.
- **Parent + Deps (g)**: parent `ddx-e34994e2`; dep `ddx-cfedee8e` with explanation that the ladder must supply MinPower for fizeau to act on.
- **Sufficient (h)**: a sub-agent given only the bead body knows which file in fizeau to read, what the gap looks like, what the negative-test outcome should be, and how to verify the end-to-end fix without asking.

### 3.2 Example of a weak bead (audit score 1/8) — annotated

`ddx-0793ac75` — *"federation: federated GraphQL resolvers + routing-metadata fields"*

Verbatim description:
```
GraphQL resolvers that aggregate across spokes (federatedWorkers,
federatedRuns, federatedDocuments, etc.). Add routing-metadata fields
exposing which spoke owns each result. Write-routing contract: hub
forwards mutations to owning node, never broadcasts (Story 15
dependency).
```

Verbatim AC:
```
1. federated* resolvers in schema.
2. Routing-metadata visible per result.
3. Mutations route to owner; tested.
4. Tests cover happy + degraded paths.
```

Failures, criterion by criterion:

- **(b) FAIL** — no problem statement (what's broken today?), no root cause, no file:line, no non-scope. The reader cannot tell where the schema lives, where resolvers go, or what "routing-metadata" should look like as a Go type.
- **(c) FAIL** — "tested" and "Tests cover happy + degraded paths" name no `Test*` function. Sub-agent has no way to know what to write or where.
- **(d) PARTIAL** — "tested" implies the resolvers are wired, but no production-call-graph assertion (e.g., "schema.graphql Mutation block exposes field X").
- **(e) FAIL** — no `go test` command, no lefthook gate.
- **(h) FAIL** — a sub-agent reading this cold cannot answer: which schema file? which resolver package? what fields on routing-metadata? what's the wire format? Hand-curation by the operator is the only path forward.

**Retrofit shape** (illustrative — not an actual edit):
```
PROBLEM
  Story 14 needs aggregate-across-spokes resolvers. Today the GraphQL
  schema (cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql) exposes only
  per-node Worker/Run/Document queries. Federation requires
  federated* counterparts that fan out to spokes and stitch results.

ROOT CAUSE
  No federated resolver layer exists. cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go
  contains only single-node resolvers. Spoke fan-out is in
  cli/internal/server/federation_spoke.go but nothing in the GraphQL
  layer calls it.

PROPOSED FIX
  - schema.graphql: add Federated{Worker,Run,Document} types with
    `ownerSpoke: String!` field.
  - graphql_resolver.go: add federatedWorkers/federatedRuns/
    federatedDocuments resolvers calling federation_spoke.FanOut.
  - graphql_mutation.go: write-routing helper routes mutations to
    owning node via federation_spoke.RouteToOwner.

NON-SCOPE
  - New non-federated resolvers (out: covered by Story 13).
  - Spoke discovery (out: covered by ddx-fe63969f).

ACCEPTANCE
  1. schema.graphql exposes federatedWorkers/federatedRuns/
     federatedDocuments queries with ownerSpoke metadata.
  2. TestFederatedWorkers_FanOutAggregates verifies fan-out + stitch.
  3. TestFederatedMutation_RoutesToOwner verifies write-routing.
  4. TestFederatedWorkers_DegradedSpoke returns partial results +
     warning.
  5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green.
  6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
```

---

## 4. Authoring checklist (paste into your editor before running `ddx bead create`)

```text
[ ] (a) Title: imperative, one line, names subsystem + change
[ ] (b) Description has all four sub-elements:
        [ ] PROBLEM (one paragraph)
        [ ] ROOT CAUSE with file:line citation (or CURRENT STATE for new features)
        [ ] PROPOSED FIX (bulleted, file:line per item)
        [ ] NON-SCOPE
[ ] (c) Each AC line is verifiable; at least one names Test* or `go test -run`
[ ] (d) New code path → AC asserts a production or test caller invokes it
[ ] (e) AC includes:
        [ ] cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green
        [ ] lefthook run pre-commit passes
[ ] (f) Labels: phase:<N>, area:<subsystem>, kind:<category>, +cross-refs
[ ] (g) Parent set; deps listed or "no deps" stated
[ ] (h) Self-test: re-read the bead from cold. Could a competent
        sub-agent execute it without asking a question? If no, retrofit.
[ ] No /tmp/ paths cited as load-bearing context. Inline the relevant
    excerpt instead — /tmp does not survive between machines.
```

---

## 5. Anti-patterns surfaced by the audit

- **`/tmp/` references as load-bearing context.** 5 of 20 audited beads cite a `/tmp/...` plan file. These are functionally broken links to a sub-agent on a different machine. **Inline the excerpt.**
- **"See parent" without inline summary.** Refactor children of `ddx-5cb6e6cd` (C4/C7/C11/C13) systematically defer detail to the parent epic. Each child fails the stand-alone test. **Inline the parent's relevant 5-10 lines.**
- **Vague test phrases.** "Tests cover X." "All execute-bead tests still pass." Both omit the actual `Test*` name or `-run` filter the executor needs.
- **Story-doc copy-paste.** One-paragraph beads (`ddx-0793ac75`, `ddx-0e5c3005`, `ddx-1d52a30c`, `ddx-0ca4ffda`) are story excerpts that were never expanded for execution. Story documents are *upstream* of beads, not the bead itself.
- **Implicit lefthook.** Lefthook runs in pre-commit hooks anyway, but if AC doesn't name it, executors omit it from their own verification before committing.

---

## 6. When to skip a criterion

data.text: I’m filing five narrower grandchildren: one for the worktree-registry CLI/tests, three for production-path audit slices, and one for the test/helper sweep plus pre-commit rule. That keeps each child inside a coherent package boundary and leaves the exact full-tree grep gate on the final audit child where it belongs.

input.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "set -euo pipefail
labels='area:config, area:refactor, kind:refactor, phase:3, initiative:zero-footprint, principle:ddx-home, decomposed'

create_bead() {
  title=\""'$1"
  desc_file="$2"
  ac_file="$3"
  ddx bead create "$title" '"\\
    --type task \\
    --priority 0 \\
    --parent ddx-e4c3d791 \\
    --labels \""'$labels" '"\\
    --description \""'$(cat "$desc_file")" '"\\
    --acceptance \""'$(cat "$ac_file")" | sed -n '"'s/"'^ID:[[:space:]]*//p'"' | head -n1
}

tmpdir="'$(mktemp -d)
trap '"'rm -rf \""'$tmpdir"'"' EXIT

cat > \""'$tmpdir/desc1.txt" <<'"'EOF'
PROBLEM
The current bead mixes a focused worktree-registry behavior change with a repo-wide path-audit sweep. That blocks execution because the registry work is small and local, while the audit spans hundreds of call sites.

ROOT CAUSE
"'`cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:166` and `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:224` already bootstrap and register the first worktree, but `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot_test.go:111` only proves the first-worktree master case. There is no operator command surface for `ddx project worktree master <path>`, so operators cannot re-pin `worktrees.json.master` even though the design requires it.

PROPOSED FIX
- `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go`: expose safe registry read/update helpers for `worktrees.json` and reuse the bootstrap lock for operator writes.
- `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot_test.go`: add `TestWorktreeRegistry_SubsequentRegisterNonMaster` covering a second checkout that registers without replacing master.
- `cli/cmd/project_worktree.go`: add `ddx project worktree list` and `ddx project worktree master <path>`.
- `cli/cmd/project_worktree_test.go`: add `TestWorktreeRegistry_OperatorRePin` that drives the CLI and inspects the rewritten registry.

NON-SCOPE
- Replacing hardcoded `.ddx` joins outside the worktree-registry command/test path.
- Wiring the repo-wide lefthook audit.

DEPS
- No deps.
EOF
cat > "$tmpdir/ac1.txt" <<'"'EOF'
1. "'`TestWorktreeRegistry_SubsequentRegisterNonMaster` passes and proves a later bootstrap appends a second `paths[]` entry without changing `master`.
